free_text: Differential cross section measurements of the
32S(d,p)33S reaction for nuclear reaction analysis
purposes
A thin self-supported target, which was prepared by
evaporating TiS2 powder onto a carbon foil of
10 ug/cm2
The detection system consisted of five
(1000-2000 um thickness) Si surface barrier detectors
(4 rotating, set at 10. degr. intervals, and 1 fixed
at 120. degr. as monitor)
Error in determination of energy is less than 2 keV.
The overall error in the absolute differential
cross section measurements. The main source of error
was the variation in the target thickness due to
carbon buildup and/or sputtering.
The statistical error was kept as low as
<2% in all cases
Data received from the author through IBANDL
Entry was approved by Dr. M.Kokkoris
SD
On. COMMON -> NOCOMMON
VS. E-LVL: KEV -> MEV.
